'''Oliver Partington''' (born 3 September 1998) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} and {{rlp|lf}} for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[England Knights]] at international level.<ref name="England Knights v Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights v Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|access-date=24 October 2019}}</ref>

He has previously played for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]], and spent time on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at the [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and the [[London Skolars]] in [[2018 RFL League 1|Betfred League 1]].

==Background==
Partington was born in [[Billinge, Merseyside]], [[England]].

==Career==
===Wigan Warriors===
In 2018 he made his [[Super League]] début for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] against the [[Castleford Tigers]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league|title=Super League: Wigan Warriors v Castleford Tigers|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
He played in the [[2020 Super League Grand Final]] which Wigan lost 8-4 against St Helens.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
On 30 May 2026, he played in Wigan's 2026 Challenge Cup final victory against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2026/jun/01/challenge-cup-crowds-are-dwindling-but-rugby-league-must-save-wembley-relationship|title=Challenge Cup crowds are dwindling but rugby league must save Wembley relationship|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
In September 2022 he signed for the [[Salford Red Devils]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63012023 |title=Salford sign Oliver Partington on two-year deal |date=23 September 2022 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>
In the 2023 Super League season, Partington played 20 matches for Salford as the club finished 7th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.salfordnow.co.uk/2023/10/02/salford-red-devils-2023-season-in-review/|title=Salford Red Devils' 2023 season in review|website=www.salfordnow.co.uk}}</ref>

===Catalans Dragons===
On 28 June 2024, it was reported that he had signed for [[Catalans Dragons]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Betfred Super League|date=28 Jun 2024|accessdate=3 Oct 2024|title=PARTINGTON JOINS THE DRAGONS IN 2025|url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/4313/partington-joins-the-dragons-in-2025}}</ref>

==International career==
In 2019 he was selected for the [[England Knights]] against [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]].<ref name="England Knights vs Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights vs Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|access-date=30 October 2019}}</ref>
